transcript.clean_text: the tour de france has been a gripping affair this year we had rain soaks chaos in nice crosswind chaos on stage seven some epic mountain passes and some thrilling bunch sprints but who have been the riders that have really sparkled at this year's tour de france it's tough to narrow it down but here's my top five the younger riders have really come to the fore at this year's tour de france and one of those to shine is mark hershey at 22 years old the swiss youngster represents some of the new talent that is sweeping into the top echelons of the sport riding for team sunweb we knew hershey had the talent winner of the under-23 world championships in 2018 and also having top 10 finishes in the big bank and deutschland tour in 2019 however it's the 2020 tour de france where hershey has really announced himself as a serious serious hitter he deserves to be on this list for his efforts on stage nine alone holding off the best riders in the world solo for 90 kilometers before getting caught inside the final 2k and still only just getting piped in the sprint it was a spectacular display of strength not letting that close defeat phase in her she kept on trying and on stage 12 to sat on the longest stage of this year's tour de france across the massive central hershey had his day launching a blistering attack on the final day's climb with 28 kilometers to go hershey wasn't getting caught this time and soloed away to take an emphatic victory winning by 47 seconds in his first tour de france no less hershey is definitely one to watch in the years to come and was definitely one of the riders of this year's tour peter sagan has been almost unbeatable in the points classification at the tour de france in the last decade so to see a rider not only challenge him but have him on the ropes is an achievement in itself sam bennett has been that rider at this year's tour consistently delivering and figuring in the top five on every bunch sprint and he also took his first tour victory on stage 10. to be so close to the win so many times is tough for any rider and it takes resolve not to lose your head sam was so close on his stage 10 victory he wasn't even sure he'd actually won and needed reassurance from the team car you saw how much the victory meant to him too showing so much emotion post race the green jersey is still to be decided with the champs-elysees beckoning on sunday for one last sprinter showdown will sam take it it's gonna be one last epic epic battle but simply for sam's grit and determination alone he deserves to be on this list we all knew pogatcha as an emerging superstar of the sport he's already finished on the podium at the royalta and taking pro wins in algarve valencia and california but the tour de france is a whole new balkan however in all honesty pogatcha has tackled his first tour de france with ease taking wins on stage 9 and 15 today pogace has looked on top of his game throughout the tour and taken classy victories in addition to that he has been a serious challenger for the yellow jersey holding second overall for most of the race despite losing over a minute to the crosswinds of stage seven pogacer is another rider with youth on his side at 21 years old he still has plenty of tours to come and we at gcn are really excited to see his performances in the years to come and i think we'll always remember the 2020 tour de france as the year when pagatcha really announced himself as a serious tour contender i don't think anyone can argue with about finance addition to this list not only has he won two stages but he has also worked tirelessly and selflessly for his leader primos roglic now that is impressive in itself but personally what i think is just so awe-inspiring is the fact that vannart took his wins in sprints beating some of the world's best bunch sprinters then in the mountains he has been one of premature glitches key mountain domestique setting the pace well well past what we would have expected from him we all knew van art was special but at this year's tour de france he has definitely announced himself as one of the world's most valuable riders someone who can not only help their leader in the mountains as a key domestic but also take victories along the way proven to be a priceless asset now if there was a contest for best nation at this year's tour the colombians would definitely be up there along with the slovenians of course but at one point there were four colombians in the top 10 on gc daniella philippe martinez took a brilliant victory on stage 13 for ef education first and michael angela lopez has been challenging every day with saturday's mountain tt to la planche de belfi still to come in this year's tour will we see lopez climb the gc even further it's going to be a thrilling battle and one which i cannot wait to watch but definitely for his win on the queen stage arguably of this year's tour de la laz he deserves to be on this list it was an emphatic victory and a dominant display of strength michelangelo lopez that is my top five but of course there is primos roglic who is looking increasingly likely to win this year's tour de france he's won the yellow jersey for the majority of the race and i think that says it in itself that he has been the top rider at this year's tour but there are still three stages to go will he keep it till paris only time will tell it's gonna be a thrilling battle which you can catch on gcn race pass don't forget geo restrictions do apply thanks for watching everyone it's been an absolutely thrilling tour de france this year and some pretty special riders have emerged from it but who did i miss off this list who you'd like to see in the top five it's been hard to do to be honest there's just there's been some amazing rider this year's tour richie port for one has battled from start to finish and deserves an honorable mention coming into this final weekend in a very respectable top five position but let me know in the comments as always thanks for watching everyone and enjoy the ride
